description: Third-party license policy matrix for AI Control Plane. Defines allowed, restricted, and conditional components for OSS/customer packaging.

scan_scope.description: Paths to scan for license boundary enforcement. Only packaging-sensitive files are included.

allowed_components.name: litellm
allowed_components.description: LiteLLM Proxy (OSS edition) - MIT licensed open source components

allowed_components.name: librechat
allowed_components.description: LibreChat - MIT licensed AI chat interface

allowed_components.name: postgresql
allowed_components.description: PostgreSQL database for LiteLLM metadata and spend logs

allowed_components.name: caddy
allowed_components.description: Caddy web server and reverse proxy

restricted_components.name: litellm-enterprise
restricted_components.description: LiteLLM Enterprise components require commercial license for production use
restricted_components.remediation: Use LiteLLM OSS edition only. Enterprise features (SSO, audit logs, enterprise admin UI) require separate commercial license.

override_policy.description: Process for approving restricted component usage
override_policy.audit_requirements: All overrides must be logged in generated reports

compliance_notes.litellm_boundary: The LiteLLM repository contains both MIT-licensed OSS code (root) and commercially-licensed enterprise code (enterprise/ subdirectory). Only OSS components may be used in customer packages without explicit enterprise agreement.
compliance_notes.verification_method: Static analysis of manifests, configs, and scripts for restricted path/content patterns. No runtime dependency scanning.
compliance_notes.update_frequency: Policy should be reviewed quarterly or when upstream license changes are detected.
